title: crossbridge-server: actionable error when slug derivation fails — point user at --slug
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

## Problem

When `crossbridge-server` runs without `--slug` and the repo has no `origin` remote configured (e.g. an unpublished local repo), it bails with an unhelpful low-level error that exposes implementation details and gives the user no actionable next step.

### Repro

```
$ crossbridge-server --group orchestration
Error: deriving slug from /home/vringar/projects/vetinari

Caused by:
    `jj git remote list` did not list an `origin` remote in /home/vringar/projects/vetinari
```

The user has to read the error twice to realize the fix is "pass `--slug <name>` manually".

## Desired behavior

When slug derivation fails (no `origin` remote in either jj or git), the error should:

1. Lead with what the user must do: pass `--slug <NAME>` explicitly.
2. Mention briefly *why* derivation failed (no `origin` remote configured) so the user understands the trigger.
3. Not expose the literal `jj git remote list` / `git remote get-url origin` command string — that is an implementation detail.

A reasonable shape:

```
Error: cannot auto-derive repo slug for /home/vringar/projects/vetinari
       (the repository has no `origin` remote configured)

       Pass `--slug <NAME>` explicitly, e.g.:
           crossbridge-server --group orchestration --slug vetinari
```

## Scope

- `crossbridge-server/src/slug.rs` — change the error returned by `jj_origin_url` / `git_origin_url` when no origin remote exists, OR (preferred) keep those low-level errors but wrap them at the `derive_from_repo` boundary with the actionable message.
- `crossbridge-server/src/main.rs` — possibly adjust the `.with_context(...)` call so the surfaced top-level message names `--slug` as the fix.
- Add/update a unit test in `slug.rs` covering the "no origin remote" path if one does not already exist.

## Out of scope

- Auto-falling-back to the directory basename. The user asked for an *explicit* demand, not silent inference.
- Refactoring how slug derivation hooks into the CLI more broadly.
